_comment: Copy to skvm.config.json and fill in paths to your local checkouts. This file is the committed template; skvm.config.json itself is gitignored so your private paths stay local. The adapters map lets src/adapters/<name>.ts resolve a user-provided source/binary tree for each external agent. Omit an adapter entry if you don't use that adapter — skvm will fall back to its default resolution order (custom config → global install on PATH → skvm-bundled, for adapters that have a bundled copy like opencode).
_providersComment: Optional. Routes SkVM's direct LLMProvider calls (compiler, bench judge, framework LLM-judge, jit-optimize eval, jit-boost candidate parsing, bare-agent adapter) from a model id to a concrete backend. Routes are matched top-to-bottom against the model id; the first glob (literal + * wildcard) match wins. If omitted or no route matches, SkVM falls back to OpenRouter with OPENROUTER_API_KEY so existing setups work unchanged. `apiKeyEnv` names an env var — keys themselves are never baked into this file.
_headlessAgentComment: Optional. Controls how jit-optimize / jit-boost spawn their headless agent (currently always opencode). `modelPrefix` is prepended to the --model argument passed to opencode, unless the model id already starts with it (idempotent). Default `"openrouter/"` preserves pre-registry behavior. Set to `"anthropic/"` to route headless agent runs through opencode's native Anthropic provider, or `""` (empty string) to pass fully-qualified ids straight through. Note: this namespace is opencode's, not SkVM's `providers.routes` namespace — they are two distinct naming systems. `providerOverride` injects OPENCODE_CONFIG_CONTENT into the subprocess to register a custom OpenAI-compatible provider without modifying the user's global opencode config. The provider `name` must match the first segment of `modelPrefix`.

headlessAgent._providerOverrideComment: Uncomment to route headless agent through a custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint instead of OpenRouter. Set modelPrefix to match the provider name (e.g. "custom/").
